twitter_thread: Made $10k in 6 months with just 2 stocks: Johnson & Johnson and Procter & Gamble.
Look for dividend aristocrats with 25+ years of dividend growth, like 3M and Coca-Cola
Use a Roth IRA to minimize tax liabilities: saved $1,200 in taxes last year
Reinvest dividends: $1,000 investment in ExxonMobil now yields $1,200/yr
Diversification trap: having 20 stocks can increase fees by $500/yr
Quarterly review: sold underperforming stock and boosted returns by 8%
pack.linkedin: I generated $10k in passive income in 6 months with just 2 stocks. The key was focusing on dividend aristocrats like Johnson & Johnson and Procter & Gamble, and using a Roth IRA to minimize tax liabilities. I also avoided over-diversification, which can increase fees by $500/yr. What specific strategies have you used to generate passive income through stocks?

Get paid $1,200 a year from a $1,000 investment
Turn $1,000 into a $10,000 passive income stream
pack.reddit_headline: Can you really build a $10k/yr passive income stream with just 2 dividend stocks?
